web.name: peer-address

web.name: pipeline-pauser-interval
web.description: The frequency on which the Pipeline Pauser component will be run to check if any pipelines need to be paused.

web.name: policy-check-filter-action
web.description: Actions in the list will go through policy check

web.name: policy-check-filter-action-skip
web.description: Actions the list will not go through policy check

web.name: policy-check-filter-http-method
web.description: API http method to go through policy check

web.name: postgres-application-name
web.description: Application name for connection tracking in pg_stat_activity.

web.name: postgres-ca-cert
web.description: CA cert file location, to verify when connecting with SSL.

web.name: postgres-client-cert
web.description: Client cert file location.

web.name: postgres-client-key
web.description: Client key file location.

web.name: postgres-connect-timeout
web.description: Dialing timeout. (0 means wait indefinitely)

web.name: postgres-database
web.description: The name of the database to use.

web.name: postgres-host
web.description: The host to connect to.

web.name: postgres-password
web.description: The user's password.

web.name: postgres-port
web.description: The port to connect to.

web.name: postgres-socket
web.description: Path to a UNIX domain socket to connect to.

web.name: postgres-sslmode
web.description: Whether or not to use SSL.

web.name: postgres-sslnegotiation
web.description: Controls how SSL encryption is negotiated with the server.

web.name: postgres-user
web.description: The user to sign in as.

web.name: prometheus-bind-ip
web.description: IP to listen on to expose Prometheus metrics.

web.name: prometheus-bind-port
web.description: Port to listen on to expose Prometheus metrics.

web.name: resource-checking-interval
web.description: Interval on which to check for new versions of resources.

web.name: resource-type-checking-interval
web.description: Interval on which to check for new versions of resource types.

web.name: resource-with-webhook-checking-interval
web.description: Interval on which to check for new versions of resources that has webhook defined.

web.name: saml-ca-cert
web.description: (Required) CA Certificate

web.name: saml-display-name
web.description: The auth provider name displayed to users on the login page

web.name: saml-email-attr
web.description: The email indicates which claim to use to map an external user email to a Concourse user email.

web.name: saml-entity-issuer
web.description: Manually specify dex's Issuer value.

web.name: saml-groups-attr
web.description: The groups key indicates which attribute to use to map external groups to Concourse teams.

web.name: saml-groups-delim
web.description: If specified, groups are returned as string, this delimiter will be used to split the group string.

web.name: saml-name-id-policy-format
web.description: Requested format of the NameID. The NameID value is mapped to the ID Token 'sub' claim.

web.name: saml-skip-ssl-validation
web.description: Skip SSL validation

web.name: saml-sso-issuer
web.description: Issuer value expected in the SAML response.

web.name: saml-sso-url
web.description: (Required) SSO URL used for POST value

web.name: saml-username-attr
web.description: The user name indicates which claim to use to map an external user name to a Concourse user name.

web.name: secret-cache-duration
web.description: If the cache is enabled, secret values will be cached for not longer than this duration (it can be less, if underlying secret lease time is smaller)

web.name: secret-cache-duration-notfound
web.description: If the cache is enabled, secret not found responses will be cached for this duration

web.name: secret-cache-enabled
web.description: Enable in-memory cache for secrets

web.name: secret-cache-purge-interval
web.description: If the cache is enabled, expired items will be removed on this interval

web.name: secret-retry-attempts
web.description: The number of attempts secret will be retried to be fetched, in case a retryable error happens.

web.name: secret-retry-interval
web.description: The interval between secret retry retrieval attempts.

web.name: session-signing-key
web.description: File containing an RSA private key, used to sign auth tokens.

web.name: signing-key-check-interval
web.description: How often to check for outdated or expired signing keys for the idtoken secrets provider

web.name: signing-key-grace-period
web.description: How long a key should still be published for the idtoken secrets provider after a new key has been generated

web.name: signing-key-rotation-period
web.description: After which time a new signing key for the idtoken secrets provider should be generated. 0 turns off generation of new keys

web.name: streaming-artifacts-compression
web.description: Compression algorithm for internal streaming.

web.name: streaming-size-limitation
web.description: Internal volume streaming size limitation in MB. In case of small limitation needed, float can be used like 0.01.

web.name: strict-transport-security
web.description: The value to set for the Strict-Transport-Security header.

web.name: syslog-address
web.description: Remote syslog server address with port (Example: 0.0.0.0:514).

web.name: syslog-ca-cert
web.description: Paths to PEM-encoded CA cert files to use to verify the Syslog server SSL cert.

web.name: syslog-drain-interval
web.description: Interval over which checking is done for new build logs to send to syslog server (duration measurement units are s/m/h; eg. 30s/30m/1h)

web.name: syslog-hostname
web.description: Client hostname with which the build logs will be sent to the syslog server.

web.name: syslog-transport
web.description: Transport protocol for syslog messages (Currently supporting tcp, udp & tls).

web.name: system-claim-key
web.description: The token claim key to use when matching system-claim-values

web.name: system-claim-value
web.description: Configure which token requests should be considered 'system' requests.

web.name: telemetry-opt-in
web.description: Enable anonymous concourse version reporting.

web.name: tls-bind-port
web.description: Port on which to listen for HTTPS traffic.

web.name: tls-ca-cert
web.description: File containing the client CA certificate, enables mTLS

web.name: tls-cert
web.description: File containing an SSL certificate.

web.name: tls-key
web.description: File containing an RSA private key, used to encrypt HTTPS traffic.

web.name: tracing-attribute
web.description: attributes to attach to traces as metadata

web.name: tracing-honeycomb-api-key
web.description: honeycomb.io api key

web.name: tracing-honeycomb-dataset
web.description: honeycomb.io dataset name

web.name: tracing-jaeger-endpoint
web.description: jaeger http-based thrift collector

web.name: tracing-jaeger-service
web.description: jaeger process service name

web.name: tracing-jaeger-tags
web.description: tags to add to the components

web.name: tracing-otlp-address
web.description: otlp address to send traces to

web.name: tracing-otlp-header
web.description: headers to attach to each tracing message

web.name: tracing-otlp-use-tls
web.description: whether to use tls or not

web.name: tracing-service-name
web.description: service name to attach to traces as metadata

web.name: tracing-stackdriver-projectid
web.description: GCP's Project ID

web.name: tsa-atc-url
web.description: ATC API endpoints to which workers will be registered.

web.name: tsa-authorized-keys
web.description: Path to file containing keys to authorize, in SSH authorized_keys format (one public key per line).

web.name: tsa-bind-ip
web.description: IP address on which to listen for SSH.

web.name: tsa-bind-port
web.description: Port on which to listen for SSH.

web.name: tsa-client-id
web.description: Client used to fetch a token from the auth server. NOTE: if you change this value you will also need to change the --system-claim-value flag so the atc knows to allow requests from this client.

web.name: tsa-client-secret
web.description: Client used to fetch a token from the auth server

web.name: tsa-cluster-name
web.description: A name for this Concourse cluster, to be displayed on the dashboard page.

web.name: tsa-debug-bind-ip
web.description: IP address on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

web.name: tsa-debug-bind-port
web.description: Port on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

web.name: tsa-garden-request-timeout
web.description: How long to wait for requests to Garden to complete. 0 means no timeout.

web.name: tsa-heartbeat-interval
web.description: interval on which to heartbeat workers to the ATC

web.name: tsa-host-key
web.description: Path to private key to use for the SSH server.

web.name: tsa-log-cluster-name
web.description: Log cluster name.

web.name: tsa-log-level
web.description: Minimum level of logs to see.

web.name: tsa-peer-address
web.description: Network address of this web node, reachable by other web nodes. Used for forwarded worker addresses.

web.name: tsa-scope
web.description: Scopes to request from the auth server

web.name: tsa-team-authorized-keys
web.description: Path to file containing keys to authorize, in SSH authorized_keys format (one public key per line).

web.name: tsa-team-authorized-keys-file
web.description: Path to file containing a YAML array of teams and their authorized SSH keys, e.g. [{team:foo,ssh_keys:[key1,key2]}].

web.name: tsa-token-url
web.description: Token endpoint of the auth server

web.name: vault-auth-backend
web.description: Auth backend to use for logging in to Vault.

web.name: vault-auth-backend-max-ttl
web.description: Time after which to force a re-login. If not set, the token will just be continuously renewed.

web.name: vault-auth-param
web.description: Paramter to pass when logging in via the backend. Can be specified multiple times.

web.name: vault-ca-cert
web.description: Path to a PEM-encoded CA cert file to use to verify the vault server SSL cert.

web.name: vault-ca-path
web.description: Path to a directory of PEM-encoded CA cert files to verify the vault server SSL cert.

web.name: vault-client-cert
web.description: Path to the client certificate for Vault authorization.

web.name: vault-client-key
web.description: Path to the client private key for Vault authorization.

web.name: vault-client-token
web.description: Client token for accessing secrets within the Vault server.

web.name: vault-client-token-path
web.description: Absolute path to a file containing the Vault client token.

web.name: vault-disable-srv-lookup
web.description: When vault URL contains NO port, use this flag to control if the client will lookup the host through DNS SRV lookup. When vault URL contains port, SRV lookup is diabled and this flag has no impact.

web.name: vault-enable-kv-mount-cache
web.description: Enable caching of KV mount version detection to reduce preflight requests to Vault.

web.name: vault-insecure-skip-verify
web.description: Enable insecure SSL verification.

web.name: vault-login-timeout
web.description: Timeout value for Vault login.

web.name: vault-lookup-templates
web.description: Path templates for credential lookup

web.name: vault-namespace
web.description: Vault namespace to use for authentication and secret lookup.

web.name: vault-path-prefix
web.description: Path under which to namespace credential lookup.

web.name: vault-query-timeout
web.description: Timeout value for Vault query.

web.name: vault-retry-initial
web.description: The initial time between retries when logging in or re-authing a secret.

web.name: vault-retry-max
web.description: The maximum time between retries when logging in or re-authing a secret.

web.name: vault-server-name
web.description: If set, is used to set the SNI host when connecting via TLS.

web.name: vault-shared-path
web.description: Path under which to lookup shared credentials.

web.name: vault-url
web.description: Vault server address used to access secrets.

web.name: web-public-dir
web.description: Web public/ directory to serve live for local development.

web.name: x-frame-options
web.description: The value to set for the X-Frame-Options header.

worker.name: baggageclaim-bind-ip
worker.description: IP address on which to listen for API traffic.

worker.name: baggageclaim-bind-port
worker.description: Port on which to listen for API traffic.

worker.name: baggageclaim-btrfs-bin
worker.description: Path to btrfs binary

worker.name: baggageclaim-debug-bind-ip
worker.description: IP address on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

worker.name: baggageclaim-debug-bind-port
worker.description: Port on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

worker.name: baggageclaim-disable-user-namespaces
worker.description: Disable remapping of user/group IDs in unprivileged volumes.

worker.name: baggageclaim-driver
worker.description: Driver to use for managing volumes.

worker.name: baggageclaim-log-level
worker.description: Minimum level of logs to see.

worker.name: baggageclaim-mkfs-bin
worker.description: Path to mkfs.btrfs binary

worker.name: baggageclaim-overlays-dir
worker.description: Path to directory in which to store overlay data

worker.name: baggageclaim-p2p-interface-family
worker.description: 4 for IPv4 and 6 for IPv6

worker.name: baggageclaim-p2p-interface-name-pattern
worker.description: Regular expression to match a network interface for p2p streaming

worker.name: baggageclaim-volumes
worker.description: Directory in which to place volume data.

worker.name: bind-ip
worker.description: IP address on which to listen for the Garden server.

worker.name: bind-port
worker.description: Port on which to listen for the Garden server.

worker.name: certs-dir
worker.description: Directory to use when creating the resource certificates volume.

worker.name: connection-drain-timeout
worker.description: Duration after which a worker should give up draining forwarded connections on shutdown.

worker.name: container-sweeper-max-in-flight
worker.description: Maximum number of containers which can be swept in parallel.

worker.name: containerd-additional-hosts
worker.description: Additional entries to add to /etc/hosts in containers. Can be specified multiple times or as a comma separated list. IP and Hostname should be separated by a space.

worker.name: containerd-allow-host-access
worker.description: Allow containers to reach the host's network. This is turned off by default.

worker.name: containerd-allowed-device
worker.description: Device cgroup rule to allow in containers, in the format 'type major:minor access' (e.g. 'b 7:* rwm' for loop devices). Can be specified multiple times.

worker.name: containerd-bin
worker.description: Path to a containerd executable (non-absolute names get resolved from $PATH).

worker.name: containerd-cni-plugins-dir
worker.description: Path to CNI network plugins. By default will set to the concourse/bin directory the concourse binary is in.

worker.name: containerd-config
worker.description: Path to a config file to use for the Containerd daemon.

worker.name: containerd-dns-proxy-enable
worker.description: Enable proxy DNS server. Note: this will enable containers to access the host network.

worker.name: containerd-dns-server
worker.description: DNS server IP address to use instead of automatically determined servers. Can be specified multiple times.

worker.name: containerd-external-ip
worker.description: IP address to use to reach container's mapped ports. Autodetected if not specified.

worker.name: containerd-init-bin
worker.description: Path to an init executable. By default will search within the concourse/bin directory the concourse binary is in.

worker.name: containerd-log-level
worker.description: Minimum level of logs to see.

worker.name: containerd-max-containers
worker.description: Max container capacity. 0 means no limit.

worker.name: containerd-mtu
worker.description: MTU size for container network interfaces. Defaults to the MTU of the interface used for outbound access by the host.

worker.name: containerd-network-pool
worker.description: Network range to use for dynamically allocated container subnets.

worker.name: containerd-oci-hooks-dir
worker.description: Path to the oci hooks dir. By default none is provided.

worker.name: containerd-privileged-mode
worker.description: How many privileges privileged containers get. full is equivalent to root on host. ignore means no extra privileges. fuse-only means enough to use fuse-overlayfs.

worker.name: containerd-request-timeout
worker.description: How long to wait for requests to Containerd to complete. 0 means no timeout.

worker.name: containerd-restricted-network
worker.description: Network ranges to which traffic from containers will be restricted. Can be specified multiple times.

worker.name: containerd-seccomp-profile
worker.description: Path to a seccomp filter override. By default will use a restrictive default set.

worker.name: containerd-v6-disable-masquerade
worker.description: Masquerade container traffic with worker address.

worker.name: containerd-v6-enable
worker.description: Enable IPv6 networking

worker.name: containerd-v6-pool
worker.description: IPv6 network range to use for dynamically allocated container addresses.

worker.name: debug-bind-ip
worker.description: IP address on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

worker.name: debug-bind-port
worker.description: Port on which to listen for the pprof debugger endpoints.

worker.name: ephemeral
worker.description: If set, the worker will be immediately removed upon stalling.

worker.name: external-garden-url
worker.description: API endpoint of an externally managed Garden server to use instead of running the embedded Garden server.

worker.name: garden-bin
worker.description: Path to a garden server executable (non-absolute names get resolved from $PATH).

worker.name: garden-config
worker.description: Path to a config file to use for the Garden backend. e.g. 'foo-bar=a,b' for '--foo-bar a --foo-bar b'.

worker.name: garden-dns-proxy-enable
worker.description: Enable proxy DNS server. Note: this will enable containers to access the host network.

worker.name: garden-max-containers
worker.description: Maximum container capacity. 0 means no limit. (default:250)

worker.name: garden-network-pool
worker.description: Network range to use for dynamically allocated container subnets. (default:10.80.0.0/16)

worker.name: garden-request-timeout
worker.description: How long to wait for requests to the Garden server to complete. 0 means no timeout.

worker.name: healthcheck-bind-ip
worker.description: IP address on which to listen for health checking requests.

worker.name: healthcheck-bind-port
worker.description: Port on which to listen for health checking requests.

worker.name: healthcheck-timeout
worker.description: HTTP timeout for the full duration of health checking.

worker.name: http-proxy
worker.description: HTTP proxy endpoint to use for containers.

worker.name: https-proxy
worker.description: HTTPS proxy endpoint to use for containers.

worker.name: log-level
worker.description: Minimum level of logs to see.

worker.name: name
worker.description: The name to set for the worker during registration. If not specified, the hostname will be used.

worker.name: no-proxy
worker.description: Blacklist of addresses to skip the proxy when reaching.

worker.name: rebalance-interval
worker.description: Duration after which the registration should be swapped to another random SSH gateway.

worker.name: resource-types
worker.description: Path to directory containing resource types the worker should advertise.

worker.name: runtime
worker.description: Runtime to use with the worker. Guardian is not available for linux/arm64. Please note that Houdini is insecure and doesn't run 'tasks' in containers.

worker.name: sweep-interval
worker.description: Interval on which containers and volumes will be garbage collected from the worker.

worker.name: tag
worker.description: A tag to set during registration. Can be specified multiple times.

worker.name: team
worker.description: The name of the team that this worker will be assigned to.

worker.name: tracing-attribute
worker.description: attributes to attach to traces as metadata

worker.name: tracing-honeycomb-api-key
worker.description: honeycomb.io api key

worker.name: tracing-honeycomb-dataset
worker.description: honeycomb.io dataset name

worker.name: tracing-jaeger-endpoint
worker.description: jaeger http-based thrift collector

worker.name: tracing-jaeger-service
worker.description: jaeger process service name

worker.name: tracing-jaeger-tags
worker.description: tags to add to the components

worker.name: tracing-otlp-address
worker.description: otlp address to send traces to

worker.name: tracing-otlp-header
worker.description: headers to attach to each tracing message

worker.name: tracing-otlp-use-tls
worker.description: whether to use tls or not

worker.name: tracing-service-name
worker.description: service name to attach to traces as metadata

worker.name: tracing-stackdriver-projectid
worker.description: GCP's Project ID

worker.name: tsa-host
worker.description: TSA host to forward the worker through. Can be specified multiple times.

worker.name: tsa-public-key
worker.description: File containing a public key to expect from the TSA.

worker.name: tsa-worker-private-key
worker.description: File containing the private key to use when authenticating to the TSA.

worker.name: version
worker.description: Version of the worker. This is normally baked in to the binary, so this flag is hidden.

worker.name: volume-sweeper-max-in-flight
worker.description: Maximum number of volumes which can be swept in parallel.

worker.name: work-dir
worker.description: Directory in which to place container data.
